zh.lifeSummary: 當Gijsbrecht I van Amstel於1145年出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德武德可可 阿 德 阿姆斯特爾時，他的父親Egbert van Amstel40歲，母親Badeloch van Muiden37歲。他與Hadewigis結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1172年被列為ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht。他去世於1201年在法國洛萊享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Amstel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Pinkus.

nameMeanings.name: Gijsbrecht
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Franz, Reinold, Wilhelm.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements gis (from gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble offspring’; see Giesel ) + berht ‘bright, famous’, a cognate of Giselbert (see Gilbert ).

variants.name: Giesel
meanings: German: from the personal name Giesel, a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names with the first element gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble offspring’. In ancient and medieval ancient Germanic society, the younger children of kings and princes were sometimes sent to be brought up at the court of a neighboring ruler, as a pledge of peace between the two nations or clans. Thus, the male personal name Gisil and its feminine equivalent Gisela mean both ‘pledge’ and ‘noble offspring’.

en.lifeSummary: When Gijsbrecht I van Amstel was born in 1145, in Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Egbert van Amstel, was 40 and his mother, Badeloch van Muiden, was 37. He married Hadewigis. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. In 1172, his occupation is listed as ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. He died in 1201, in Lorraine, France, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gijsbrecht I van Amstel est né en 1145, à Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Egbert van Amstel, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Badeloch van Muiden, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Hadewigis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. En 1172, sa profession de son indiquée est ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. Il est décédé en 1201, à Lorraine, France, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gijsbrecht I van Amstel 1145 in Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Egbert van Amstel 40 und seine Mutter Badeloch van Muiden 37. Er hat Hadewigis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1172 ist sein Beruf als ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht aufgeführt. Er starb 1201 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Lothringen, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gijsbrecht I van Amstel è nato il nel 1145 nel Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Egbert van Amstel, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Badeloch van Muiden, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Hadewigis. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1172, la sua occupazione è indicata come ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. Morì nel 1201, nel Lorraine, Francia, all'età di 56 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Gijsbrecht I van Amstel(이)가 1145년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 우더커크 아아 데 아스텔에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Egbert van Amstel(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Badeloch van Muiden(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Hadewigis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1172년, 그의 직업은 ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht이었다. 그는 1201년 프랑스 로레이인에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gijsbrecht I van Amstel nasceu em 1145, em Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Egbert van Amstel, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Badeloch van Muiden, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Hadewigis. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1172, sua profissão aparece como ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. Ele faleceu em 1201, em Lorraine, França, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gijsbrecht I van Amstel родился в 1145 г. в местности оудиркирк аан де амстел, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Egbert van Amstel было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Badeloch van Muiden, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Hadewigis. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. В 1172 году он работал по профессии ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. Он умер в 1201 г. в местности лорреаин, Франция в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gijsbrecht I van Amstel nació en 1145, en Ouderkerk aan de Amstel, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Egbert van Amstel, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Badeloch van Muiden, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Hadewigis. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. En 1172, su ocupación registrada era ministeriaal van de bisschop van utrecht. Murió en 1201, en Lorraine, Francia, a la edad de 56 años.
